original_reasoning: Husband/wife/pair/couple vs Zeugma (city named for 'bridge/yoking'). Zeugnumi means 'to yoke/join'; Arabic zawj means 'pair/couple/spouse'. Both share core meaning of 'joining/pairing two together'. Clear semantic connection.
Arabs (Arab people) vs to clatter/chatter/make Arab-like sounds. Greek verb derived from 'Arab' to denote their speech style. Direct etymological/semantic link.
Euphorbia plant (medicinal/purging) vs Euphorbus (figure in Iliad, whose name means 'well-fed'). Arabic is clearly a loanword from Euphorbus/Euphorbia. Direct botanical-nominal link.

Stupid confused person/fighting vs magician/mage/sorcerer. The Arabic Maj (مَاجَ) relates to the Magi (Magus), Persian priestly/scholarly class. Conceptual overlap through the Magi who were also seen as tricksters/conjurers.
eyes tearing/weeping vs oblique form of tear/teardrop — direct semantic match: tears
a deep well/abyss in Hadhramaut where souls of the wicked gather vs a gulf/cleft/pit at Athens where criminals were thrown — both are named abyssal pits with religious/judicial significance
Arabic: speech/discourse — Greek: speaking — clear semantic match
Arabic explicitly includes the first coming of wind or rain, and Greek means having come, so the arrival concept matches directly.
